intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A supportive, motivational voice (vs. clinical data-focused) will increase daily engagement with the health dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality: Friendly/Motivational vs. Professional/Clinical
facade.label: Encouraging coach tone
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, supportive language; celebrates small wins; uses 'you've got this' energy
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, factual tone; wants data clarity over motivation; finds coaching language annoying or patronizing

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable visual containers over sharp-cornered layouts
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore more organic, curve-based visual language for data display and metric containers
facade.rejectImplies: Consider sharper geometry, grid-based layouts, or minimalist rectangles for a more structured, clinical feel

evidence.hypothesis: A supportive, motivational voice (vs. clinical data-focused) will increase daily engagement with the health dashboard
evidence.implication: User prefers warm, supportive language; celebrates small wins; uses 'you've got this' energy

facade.hypothesis: Warm, organic transitions create a calming atmosphere that makes health tracking feel nurturing rather than clinical
facade.label: Soft gradients
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward warm, inviting mood—gentle onboarding and soothing color language
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler tones or sharper contrast—possibly more minimal or clinical aesthetic for data clarity

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer quantified metrics (count, percentage, days) over narrative summaries for tracking health experiments
facade.label: Number badges
facade.acceptImplies: Show progress as prominent numeric displays (e.g., '12 days', '73%', '5 experiments') rather than prose descriptions
facade.rejectImplies: Favor narrative or qualitative descriptions over hard numbers; use text-based summaries like 'On track' or 'Good progress' instead of numeric callouts

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking multiple health experiments need constant access to experiment list without losing their current view
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: persistent sidebar vs collapsible vs tab-based
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick context-switching between experiments and seeing full list at a glance; design should accommodate persistent left column even on mobile
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers focused single-experiment view; sidebar should hide or collapse to maximize space for current data; navigation happens via top tabs or hamburger menu

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid animated transitions between dashboard sections over instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth page slides
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use continuous slide/fade animations when switching between experiment views, data tables, or settings—motion creates continuity and aids spatial memory of where data lives
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should use instant page swaps or no-animation state changes—faster perceived performance and simpler interactions take priority over visual flow

facade.hypothesis: A lighthearted, game-like voice with gentle humor will encourage consistent tracking without feeling like medical obligation
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: playful vs serious
facade.label: Playful tone
facade.acceptImplies: User wants personality and delight in their health tool; may prefer streak systems, celebratory language, emoji or playful microcopy over clinical terminology
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes clarity and seriousness; voice should be neutral and data-focused; playfulness feels frivolous in a health context

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for logging and monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking and experiment cards.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term wellness projects

Active Experiments

Morning Meditation

Daily 10-minute practice

68% complete 34 of 50 days

Sleep Optimization

Target 8 hours nightly

45% complete 23 of 50 days
Overview
5
Total Experiments
2
